{"key_event":"Labor mobilization and Santa María massacre","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened as party machines, labor newspapers, mutual-aid societies, mancomunales, strikes, and mass protests expanded, though the 1907 massacre sharply checked organized labor in the short run. State power remained very strong and edged up because nitrate revenues, territorial settlements, conscription, military professionalization, and coercive repression offset parliamentary cabinet instability and the temporary disruption of the Valparaíso earthquake.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":7.2,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.4}
